== Notes for [[Item:Q99764|Q99764]] ==

The earliest translator of the “Almagest” into Arabic was the Jew Sahl al-Tabari. Another translation was made a little later (in 829), on the basis of a Syrian version, by al Hajjaj ibn Yusuf. Also called '''Rabban al-Tabari''', meaning the Rabbi of Tiberias. Flourished about the beginning of the ninth century. Jewish astronomer and physician. The first translator of the Almagest into Arabic.

H. Suter: Die Mathematiker und Astronomen der Araber (l0, 1900);

M. Steinschneider: Die arabische Literatur der Juden (23-34, Frankfurt, 1902).

http://www.usc.edu/schools/college/crcc/private/cmje/heritage/History_of_Islamic_Science.pdf

REFERENCE_NUMBERS:
67051AD770E271EFBBFA9822EF79916CE376
geni:6000000035172499101

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
